mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, systematically compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hiking periods with t-statistic > 1.96, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE jumps exceed 0.3pp, or signal loses predictive power after 2008 financial crisis onset.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of high inflation indicates supply constraints that erode corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth coincides with positive 20-day equity returns in over 65% of observations during high inflation periods, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, borrowing costs severely constrain corporate leverage and consumer credit, generating negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Equity markets produce positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS surpasses 5.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm a tightening labor market that boosts corporate revenue expectations, yielding positive returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns in over 50% of cases, or the interaction effect shows negative economic significance.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly reflects aggregate economic output and corporate earnings potential, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth coincides with negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during disinflationary periods signals improving manufacturing efficiency and margin expansion, driving positive equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth during low inflation fails to generate positive 5-day returns in over 60% of instances, or the regime-specific predictive power disappears after 2010.
